raw_response:
Root resorption typically begins during infancy, specifically by about 6-7 months old when deciduous teeth begin forming roots. Therefore, considering the context of dental development rather than general bone physiology:
\boxed{\text{C}} Based on the typical timing of tooth root formation and maturation starting around 6-7 months post-birth, option C aligns best with current understanding regarding the onset of root resorption in children's dentition. The other ages listed do not correspond accurately to this process in the oral cavity where it becomes significant. Thus, I select \boxed{C}.
